perturbation.original_text: b) "SERVICES PERIOD" means that period of time commencing upon the full execution of this Agreement by both Parties and terminating on May 31, 2014 unless sooner terminated under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: b) "SERVICES PERIOD" means that period of time commencing upon the full execution of this Agreement by both Parties and terminating on May 31, 2015 unless sooner terminated under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the termination date of the 'SERVICES PERIOD' from May 31, 2014 to May 31, 2015, it creates a contradiction with Section 2(a) and Schedule A, which explicitly state that Auriemma will provide services during the Services Period as defined in Section 1(b). However Section 4(b), specifies payment dates until June 1, 2015. Now with the extension until 2015, it becomes ambiguous whether Auriemma is obligated to provide services after May 31, 2014. The term 'SERVICES PERIOD' definition is now creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the Contract Period: a) Subject to the terms of Paragraph 6 below, Auriemma will make the appearances and provide to Berkshire during the Services Period the services, initiatives and programs described in Schedule A attached hereto (the "Endorsement Services"); and b) Auriemma grants to Berkshire the exclusive right and license (the "License Rights") to use the Auriemma Identification during the Contract Period and throughout the Contract Territory solely in connection with the advertisement and promotion of Berkshire and the Financial Services; c) The License Rights are exclusive to Berkshire and may not be assigned or in any way conveyed by Berkshire without Auriemma's express written consent, except in the event of a merger by Berkshire with another entity offering Banking Services.
perturbation.justification: The modification extends the 'SERVICES PERIOD' from May 31, 2014, to May 31, 2015. This creates an in-text contradiction because the 'Contradicted Text' section states that Auriemma will provide services 'during the Services Period,' which, according to the original definition, ended in 2014. The extension implies a continued service obligation that conflicts with the original terms.

perturbation.original_text: 7. AURIEMMA'S APPROVAL. a) Berkshire shall use the Auriemma Identification only in such a form and manner as is specifically approved by Auriemma and, upon the reasonable request by Auriemma, shall use any reasonable legends, markings, and notices of trademark rights or registration reasonably specified by Auriemma, or any other notice of Auriemma's ownership, including copyright. b) Berkshire agrees that all use of the Auriemma Identification in connection with advertising, displays, and other materials and all advertising shall not be made unless and until finished samples of such proposed use have been provided to Auriemma and such use has been approved by Auriemma or Auriemma's authorized representative. Auriemma agrees that any use of the Auriemma Identification submitted for approval as provided herein will be deemed to have been approved by Auriemma if the same is not disapproved in writing within ten (10) business days after receipt Source: BERKSHIRE HILLS BANCORP INC, 10-Q, 8/9/2012 thereof. If any use of the Auriemma Identification submitted for approval as provided herein is disapproved, Berkshire shall be advised of the specific grounds for disapproval. Subject to this Agreement, Berkshire agrees to follow Auriemma's reasonable instructions and guidelines regarding proper usage of the Auriemma Identification in all respects as may have been reasonably and timely provided to Berkshire by the Auriemma.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. AURIEMMA'S APPROVAL. a) Berkshire shall use the Auriemma Identification only in such a form and manner as it deems appropriate. Auriemma may, at his discretion, provide feedback. b) Berkshire is not required to submit materials using Auriemma Identification for approval, but will consider feedback from Auriemma if provided. If Berkshire does not receive feedback within ten (10) business days, the use is automatically approved. Subject to this Agreement, Berkshire will consider Auriemma's suggestions regarding proper usage of the Auriemma Identification.
perturbation.explanation: This change significantly weakens Auriemma's control over the use of his 'Auriemma Identification'. Previously, Section 7 stated that Berkshire *shall* use the identification only in a manner approved by Auriemma. The revised version allows Berkshire to use the identification as it deems fit, merely stating Auriemma *may* provide feedback. This creates an in-text contradiction with section 8, which implies a mutual effort to protect the 'Auriemma Identification'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. PROTECTION OF THE AURIEMMA IDENTIFICATION. Berkshire and Auriemma agree that they will take all necessary steps during the Contract Period and thereafter to protect the Auriemma Identification in connection with the Endorsement Services.
perturbation.justification: The original text of Section 7 gives Auriemma control and approval over how Berkshire uses the 'Auriemma Identification.' The changed text gives Berkshire sole discretion, merely considering feedback from Auriemma. This directly contradicts Section 8, which states 'Berkshire and Auriemma agree that they will take all necessary steps...to protect the Auriemma Identification.' The change removes Auriemma's ability to enforce protection of the identification, creating a strong, obvious conflict within the contract, thus making it fall under the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 10. TERMINATION AND DEFAULT. a) TERMINATION FOR BREACH. Either Party shall have the right, without prejudice to any other rights it may have, to terminate this Agreement if the other Party materially breaches its obligation hereunder and such breach remains uncured. A material breach occurs if either Party (i) fails to make any payment, or (ii) fails to observe or perform any of the covenants, agreements, or obligations (other than payments of money). Upon the breach of either of the above conditions, the non-defaulting party may terminate this Agreement as follows: (A) as to a default under clause (i) above, if payment is not made within ten (10) days after the defaulting party shall have received written notice of such failure to make payment; or (B) as to a default under clause (ii) above, if such default is not cured within thirty (30) days after the defaulting party shall have received written notice specifying in reasonable detail the nature of such default and such action the defaulting party must take in order to cure each such item of default.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. TERMINATION AND DEFAULT. a) TERMINATION FOR BREACH. Either Party *may* have the right, without prejudice to any other rights it may have, to terminate this Agreement if the other Party materially breaches its obligation hereunder and such breach remains uncured. A material breach *may* occur if either Party (i) fails to make any payment, or (ii) fails to observe or perform any of the covenants, agreements, or obligations (other than payments of money). Upon the breach of either of the above conditions, the non-defaulting party may terminate this Agreement as follows: (A) as to a default under clause (i) above, if payment is not made within ten (10) days after the defaulting party shall have received written notice of such failure to make payment; or (B) as to a default under clause (ii) above, if such default is not cured within thirty (30) days after the defaulting party shall have received written notice specifying in reasonable detail the nature of such default and such action the defaulting party must take in order to cure each such item of default.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall have the right' to 'may have the right' and 'A material breach occurs' to 'A material breach may occur' introduces significant ambiguity regarding termination rights. While subsections (A) and (B) still outline specific conditions for termination following a default, the initial modification makes it uncertain whether any breach automatically grants termination rights. This creates an in-text contradiction because the detailed cure periods imply a right to terminate, but the modified opening statement suggests termination is discretionary, now consider a scenario where one party materially breaches the agreement, but the other party chooses not to terminate it immediately. The question arises: does the non-breaching party waive their right to terminate in the future if the breach continues or worsens? The lack of clarity on the enforceability of the agreement creates uncertainty and the term 'TERMINATION AND DEFAULT' definition is now creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12. OTHER REMEDIES AND RIGHTS. The termination rights set forth herein shall not constitute the exclusive remedy of the non-defaulting party. Termination in accordance with the above provisions shall be without prejudice to any rights or claims, which the terminating party may otherwise have against the defaulting party. In the event of any arbitration or litigation, including breach, enforcement or interpretation, arising out of this Agreement, the prevailing party Source: BERKSHIRE HILLS BANCORP INC, 10-Q, 8/9/2012 of such litigation shall be entitled to recover reasonable attorney's fees, costs, and expenses, including pre-litigation and appellate attorneys' fees and costs.
perturbation.justification: The modification of 'shall have the right' to 'may have the right' and 'A material breach occurs' to 'A material breach may occur' introduces ambiguity regarding termination rights, directly contradicting the language in section 12, which states, 'The termination rights set forth herein shall not constitute the exclusive remedy of the non-defaulting party.' By making the termination right discretionary, the change introduces uncertainty regarding the enforceability of that right. The detailed cure periods in section 10 also imply a right to terminate. The change follows the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' by making the obligation of termination discretionary instead of mandatory.
